name: The Castle of the Counts of Benavente from Brime de Urz – circular through the Sierra de Carpurias
name_original: El Castillo de los condes de Benavente desde Brime de Urz – circular por la Sierra de Carpurias
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Quintanilla De Urz are set within a lush valley in the north of Zamora province, characterized by diverse and uneven terrain. The region is watered by the Arroyo Almucera, contributing to varied landscapes suitable for cycling. Elevated points offer views over the Órbigo valley, providing a mix of physical activity and cultural immersion along historical routes. Best touring cycling routes around Quintanilla De Urz The most popular touring cycling route is Tera Valley Loop, a 35.0 miles (56.3 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete. This route explores the scenic Tera Valley. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía de la Plata and Camino del Sureste Loop, a moderate 31.9 miles (51.3 km) path. This route follows sections of the historic Vía de la Plata, offering a connection to ancient travel paths. Local touring cyclists also love the The Castle of the Counts of Benavente from Brime de Urz – circular through the Sierra de Carpurias, a 29.1 miles (46.8 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Carpurias, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Touring cycling around Quintanilla De Urz is defined by its uneven terrain, lush valleys, and historical routes like the Vía de la Plata. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Quintanilla De Urz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Quintanilla De Urz?
A: There are 5 touring cycling routes documented on komoot for the Quintanilla De Urz area. These routes offer a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult options to explore the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Quintanilla De Urz?
A: The region around Quintanilla De Urz is characterized by its diverse and uneven terrain, nestled within a lush valley watered by the Arroyo Almucera. You can expect varied landscapes, from valley floors to elevated points offering views over the Órbigo valley, providing both physical challenge and scenic rewards.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride?
A: Yes, there is 1 easy touring cycling route available. While the overall terrain is uneven, this option caters to those looking for a less strenuous experience, allowing you to enjoy the beautiful natural surroundings at a comfortable pace.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while cycling in Quintanilla De Urz?
A: The area is rich in history. You can cycle across the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Vizana&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the ancient Roman road and the Vía de la Plata. Another significant stop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Marta de Tera&lt;/a&gt;, located on the Camino Sanabrés, known for its equinox phenomenon. For a rewarding detour, consider cycling towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel&lt;/a&gt; in Alija del Infantado for panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037068130/tera-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tera Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037627997/via-de-la-plata-and-camino-del-sureste-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata and Camino del Sureste Loop&lt;/a&gt; are both excellent circular options that showcase the region's beauty and history.

Q: What is the most challenging touring cycling route around Quintanilla De Urz?
A: The most challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383963597/castro-de-las-labradas-and-alija-del-infantado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Labradas and Alija del Infantado Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers approximately 21.8 miles (35 km) and features significant elevation changes, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: What natural features can I explore on a touring bike in this region?
A: The region boasts beautiful natural areas, including lush valleys and the Arroyo Almucera. You can explore the scenic Tera Valley on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037068130/tera-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tera Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, you might encounter geological formations such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5572341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prehistoric Rock Formation of Arrabalde&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that follow historical paths like the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, the region is intersected by historical routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037627997/via-de-la-plata-and-camino-del-sureste-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata and Camino del Sureste Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically follows sections of the historic Vía de la Plata, which is also part of the Camino de Santiago network, offering a unique connection to centuries of pilgrimage and travel.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Quintanilla De Urz?
A: The routes in Quintanilla De Urz are appreciated by the komoot community, with over 20 touring cyclists having explored the varied terrain. Riders often highlight the blend of physical activity with cultural immersion, particularly along the historical routes and through the picturesque valleys.

Q: Can I find places to explore beyond the cycling routes themselves?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the routes, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alija del Infantado Town Center&lt;/a&gt;, known for its traditional architecture. There are also historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5966274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de San Adrián&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Las Labradas&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's ancient past.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Sierra de Carpurias?
A: Yes, the route titled &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037613425/the-castle-of-the-counts-of-benavente-from-brime-de-urz-circular-through-the-sierra-de-carpurias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Castle of the Counts of Benavente from Brime de Urz – circular through the Sierra de Carpurias&lt;/a&gt; takes you through this area, offering scenic views and a diverse cycling experience within the Sierra de Carpurias.

Q: What is the typical duration for a touring cycling route in this region?
A: The duration of routes varies depending on their length and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037068130/tera-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tera Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours and 56 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383275431/the-benavente-river-vegas-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Benavente river vegas — Benavente&lt;/a&gt; route can take approximately 4 hours and 18 minutes.
tips: Brime de Urz is a small town south of the Sierra De Carpurias. The most notable monument in the city is the Ermita de San Esteban, which stands on top of a hill overlooking the city.
A prehistoric stone circle. The structure was usually built from locally quarried granite or slate. They were used for various ceremonies throughout the seasons.

Ruins of a palace and castle built in the 15th century by members of the Escobar family, who were the lords of the town. Of the Palace, now in a state of abandonment, the outer masonry walls have been preserved. The castle is visible from the road.

Ruins of a fortified settlement from the time of the Celts who fought the Romans when they arrived in this area of the Iberian Peninsula. The Castro de las Labradas is located on a steep hill and you will have to bike uphill or push.
A small town in the northeast of Zamora near the Sierra de Carpurias and the Eria river.

waypoints.name: Ermita de San Esteban de Brime de Urz
